2605.10574.title: LLM Jaggedness Unlocks Scientific Creativity
2605.10574.paper_summary: As artificial intelligence advances, models are not improving uniformly. Instead, progress unfolds in a jagged fashion, with capabilities growing unevenly across tasks, domains, and model scales. In this work, we examine this dynamic jaggedness through the lens of scientific idea generation. We introduce SciAidanBench, a benchmark of open-ended scientific questions designed to measure the scientific creativity of large language models (LLMs). Given a scientific question, models are asked to generate as many unique and coherent ideas as possible, with the total number of valid responses serving as a proxy for creative potential. Evaluating 19 base models across 8 providers (30 total variants including reasoning versions), we find that jaggedness manifests both across models and within models. First, in a cross-task comparison between general and scientific creativity, improvements in general creativity do not translate uniformly to scientific creativity, revealing divergent capability profiles across models. Second, at the prompt level, stronger models do not improve uniformly; instead, they exhibit high variability, with bursts of creativity on some questions and limited performance on others. Third, at the domain level, individual models display uneven strengths across scientific subfields, reflecting fragmented internal capability profiles. Finally, we show that this jaggedness can be harnessed. We explore mechanisms of inference-time compute, knowledge pooling, and brainstorming to combine models effectively and construct meta-model ensembles that outperform any single model. Our results position jaggedness not as a limitation, but as a resource, a structural feature of AI progress that, when understood and leveraged, can amplify LLM-driven scientific creativity.
2605.10574.paper_summary_zh: 隨著人工智慧的進步，模型的改進並不是均勻的。相反，進展以不規則的方式展開，能力在任務、領域和模型規模之間不均勻增長。在這項工作中，我們通過科學創意生成的視角來檢視這種動態的不規則性。我們介紹了SciAidanBench，這是一個開放式科學問題的基準，旨在衡量大型語言模型（LLMs）的科學創造力。給定一個科學問題，模型被要求生成儘可能多的獨特且連貫的想法，總有效回應的數量作為創造潛力的代理指標。我們評估了來自8個提供者的19個基礎模型（包括推理版本在內的30個變體），發現不規則性在模型之間和模型內部都有體現。首先，在一般創造力和科學創造力之間的跨任務比較中，一般創造力的提升並不均勻地轉化為科學創造力，揭示了模型之間的能力差異配置。其次，在提示層面上，較強的模型並不均勻改善；相反，它們表現出高變異性，在某些問題上創造力爆發，而在其他問題上表現有限。第三，在領域層面上，個別模型在科學子領域中顯示出不均勻的優勢，反映出內部能力配置的碎片化。最後，我們展示了這種不規則性可以被利用。我們探索了推理時間計算、知識聚合和頭腦風暴的機制，以有效結合模型並構建超越任何單一模型的元模型集成。我們的結果將不規則性定位為一種資源，而不是限制，作為AI進步的一個結構特徵，當被理解和利用時，可以放大LLM驅動的科學創造力。
2605.10574.author: Shray Mathur et.al.
2605.10574.authors: Shray Mathur, J. Anibal Boscoboinik, Esther H. R. Tsai, Kevin G. Yager
